summary: All blocking gates pass (lint, typecheck, 344 vitest tests). The prior review's required repair — the dual-route-tree loading-state bug in AppLayout — has been correctly fixed in commit 571c0f5. AppLayout now uses a single Routes tree, destructures both isAuthenticated and isLoading from useConvexAuth(), and delegates auth gating to ProtectedRoute/AdminRoute. All contract files, exports, signatures, and invariants are satisfied. The provider nesting uses ConvexAuthProvider (from @convex-dev/auth/react) which subsumes ConvexProvider — functionally correct per the library's API despite the contract specifying separate providers.
why_previous_attempt_failed: The prior review (attempt 1-2) identified that AppLayout split routes into two trees based on isAuthenticated without checking isLoading first. During auth initialization (isLoading=true, isAuthenticated=false), protected routes would hit a catch-all redirect to /login instead of showing ProtectedRoute's spinner. Commit 571c0f5 fixed this by refactoring to a single route tree where ProtectedRoute handles the loading/auth gating directly.

broken_contract.issue: No contract violations remain. All files, exports, signatures, and invariants match the contract.

pattern_compatibility.candidate_pattern: Single route tree with ProtectedRoute/AdminRoute as per-route wrappers (current implementation)
pattern_compatibility.reason: This pattern correctly delegates loading-state and auth-check logic to ProtectedRoute (isLoading → spinner, !isAuthenticated → redirect) and AdminRoute (auth check first, then role query with skip, then role check). AppLayout only conditionally renders TopNav based on isAuthenticated && !isLoading, keeping the nav from flashing during initialization. This matches the contract invariant that protected routes show a spinner during loading.
pattern_compatibility.rejected_pattern: none — the prior dual-route-tree pattern has been removed
